steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The “but” signals a contrast: the habitat explanation clashes with the fact that the region is “notoriously inaccessible.” This suggests the absence might be due to insufficient exploration of the region, not unsuitable habitat.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Inaccessibility suggests the region hasn't been thoroughly explored, making the “perceived absence” a product of incomplete searching rather than unsuitable habitat. A focuses on species confusion, B reverses the relationship (limited access yields fewer observed species, not more), and D simply restates the habitat explanation the “but” is meant to question. That matches choice C — C is correct.
